raw_response:
I agree with the summary provided. Option C correctly captures the essence of the working time for an impression material—it spans from the initiation of mixing up until shortly before the point where elasticity diminishes significantly making it no longer manipulable. This aligns well with standard definitions used in dentistry regarding how long clinicians have to accurately form impressions without compromising quality.

Therefore, my answer remains: C.
